titleStmt.title: SII 25.106: original edition by M. D. Sampath
No. 106. (A.R. No. 106 of 1908.) TIRUPPATTŪR, TIRUPPATTUR TALUK, RAMANATHAPURAM DISTRICT.

summary.p: North wall of the maṇḍapa in front of the central shrine in the Tiruttaḷīśvara temple.
This fragmentary record may be assigned on palaeographical grounds to c. 12-13th Century. The exact date is not clear as the year opposite the 14th year is lost.

The record seems to mention the money deposited into the temple treasury by a lady Śaṅkaranārāyaṉaṉ Tirunaṅgai for the purpose of some offerings to the deity in the temple of Tirultaḷiyāṇḍār at Tirupputtūr, a brahmadēya village. The donatrix mentioned here is identical with her namesake referred to in another record of Parākrama-pāṇḍya discussed below. (vide. No. 107 below) The details are not clear because the beginning and the end of the lines are lost. Since the donatrix of this record also figures in a record of the 20th regnal year of Parākrama-Pāṇḍya, the king of the present inscription may be identified with him.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
—māyāṇṭiyadevaṟkku yāṇṭu 10 4 ĀvatiṉThe beginning of the lines are lost.
—brahmadeyan tirupputtūrt tiruttaḷiyāṇṭāṟ
—ṣṇuviṉ brāhmaṇi caṅkara nārāyaṇa ntirunaṅkai
—koṉṟukkum Indevar śrī paṇṭārattile nittam cūla Uḻa
—śrīpaṇṭārttu Ivviruvarum Oṭukkiṉa coḻiya naṟpaḻaṅkācu
—ṭiyāle Inniman=tañ celluvatākka kalveṭivittom mula
Digital edition of SII 25.106 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
